subject: Application for Dark Web Threat Analyst
body_paragraphs: I am applying for the Dark Web Threat Analyst role at Resecurity. My background combines cybersecurity research, OSINT, Python automation, CTF infrastructure, and structured technical writing, with a focus on turning fragmented technical evidence into clear findings. Resecurity’s work in cybercrime monitoring, digital risk, and adversary activity aligns with the investigative work I have built through security research, offensive tooling, and hands-on analysis projects.

At Dana Incorporated, I worked as a Cybersecurity Engineer Intern building Python/FastAPI key-rotation middleware integrated with Azure Managed HSM for secure ECU key-management workflows. I also performed CAN/UDS on-target security testing aligned with ISO/SAE 21434 and wrote reusable test cases and onboarding documentation adopted by the team. That experience strengthened how I validate technical evidence, document repeatable procedures, and communicate security findings in a way that other analysts and engineers can use.

My CTF and research background is especially relevant to threat intelligence workflows that require source validation, adversary emulation awareness, and multi-source correlation. I built and managed self-hosted CTF infrastructure for GrizzHacks8, authored challenges with public source and reproducible solver paths, and have contributed CTF challenges and infrastructure for events supporting 1,000+ cumulative participants. I also placed 1st in Hack The Box Data Dystopia at DEF CON 32 with Team L3ak and rank in the top 1% on TryHackMe/Hack The Box. These environments have given me practical exposure to attacker tradecraft, credential abuse patterns, enumeration logic, exploitation chains, and evidence-driven reporting without relying on unsupported assumptions.

I would bring Resecurity a practical technical toolkit: Python, Go, Bash, SQL, Burp Suite, Nmap, Wireshark, Ghidra, Metasploit, Nessus/OpenVAS, Hashcat/John, impacket/NetExec, Sliver, Volatility, and familiarity with MITRE ATT&CK, NIST SP 800-61, OWASP Top 10, TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP(S), SMB, Kerberos, LDAP, TLS, and PKI. My current independent lab work through Fortify Vector Labs focuses on AppSec research, red-team tooling, CTF infrastructure, and AI-security experimentation, while my AutoRT project explores policy-driven multi-agent assessment workflows for reconnaissance, enumeration, vulnerability analysis, evidence collection, reporting, and remediation. For this role, I would apply that same disciplined approach to monitoring cybercrime ecosystems, researching ransomware and credential theft activity, correlating OSINT signals, and producing structured intelligence for stakeholders.
closing: Thank you for your time and consideration; I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my research and automation background can support Resecurity’s threat intelligence mission.
fit_summary: Cybersecurity research background supported by AppSec research, CTF infrastructure, offensive tooling, cryptanalysis, and automotive security testing.
Direct alignment with required OSINT, Python automation, threat intelligence, MITRE ATT&CK awareness, and clear written communication.
Evidence of structured reporting and documentation from Dana internship, CTF write-ups, reproducible solver paths, and onboarding/test documentation.
Hands-on familiarity with attacker tradecraft through CTFs, offensive tooling, enumeration, credential-focused tools, and security automation.

Resecurity is described in the input as a threat intelligence company focused on cybercrime monitoring, digital risk, and adversary activity.

Candidate is familiar with MITRE ATT&CK, NIST SP 800-61, OWASP Top 10, TCP/IP, DNS, HTTP(S), SMB, Kerberos, LDAP, TLS, and PKI.
Candidate’s Fortify Vector Labs work focuses on AppSec research, red-team tooling, CTF infrastructure, and AI-security experimentation.
Candidate’s AutoRT project is a policy-driven multi-agent red-team assessment PoC for recon, enumeration, vulnerability analysis, evidence collection, reporting, and remediation workflows.
warnings: The provided profile does not show direct professional dark web monitoring experience; the letter frames cybercriminal ecosystem familiarity as research-oriented and adjacent.
No web search was used, so company details are limited to the supplied job_info context.
Some candidate experiences have dates in 2025-2026; they are treated as valid because the requested date is 2026-05-19.
